rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL model for AGREE expressions and declarations
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are inserted after relevant subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
